title: BMAD COPILOT
subtitle: BMAD wizard to create a structured and operational project

brief.title: Brief
brief.helper: Define the problem, objectives and context.

mapping.title: Mapping
mapping.helper: List actors, resources, risks and dependencies.

delivery.title: Delivery
delivery.helper: Plan execution, milestones and validation criteria.

suggestions.title: Contextual suggestions

brief.0: Clarify the primary goal in one measurable sentence.
brief.2: Identify the main decision maker and stakeholders.

mapping.0: Map critical dependencies and associated risks.
mapping.1: Link each actor to concrete responsibilities.

architecture.0: Define interfaces and contracts between components.
architecture.1: Document trade-offs on cost, timeline, complexity.
architecture.2: Validate failure points and mitigation plans.

delivery.0: Frame milestones with clear Definition of Done.
delivery.1: Prioritize deliverables by impact and risk.
delivery.2: Attach success metrics to each milestone.

suggestions.softExtra: Pro suggestion: schedule a weekly BMAD risk review.
suggestions.strongExtra: Pro+ suggestion: prepare a What-If scenario for the most critical decision.

form.reloadSubtitle: Select an existing project to restore the wizard.
form.deliveryPlaceholder: Milestones, definition of done, timeline, risks...
form.freeNotesBriefPlaceholder: Additional context, prior decisions, non-negotiable constraints, useful references...
form.freeNotesMappingPlaceholder: Business trade-offs, implicit dependencies, assumptions to validate, coordination points...
form.freeNotesArchitecturePlaceholder: Technical constraints, accepted debt, architecture decisions, discarded alternatives...
form.freeNotesDeliveryPlaceholder: Mitigation plan, launch prerequisites, execution details, quality checks...

ui.controlsHint: User mode = guidance depth | Telemetry = data sharing level | Proactive mode = automatic suggestion intensity.

userMode.title: User mode
userMode.description: Sets the guidance style. Normal keeps control manual; Pro enables more initiative and recommendations.

telemetryMode.title: BMAD telemetry
telemetryMode.description: Defines where usage signals are kept: local only, or remote sharing based on your choice.

proactiveMode.title: Proactive mode
proactiveMode.description: Controls how often auto-suggestions appear: manual only, soft proactive, or strong proactive.

infoPage.title: What is BMAD?
infoPage.openAria: Learn more about the BMAD method
infoPage.whatIs: BMAD (Brief, Mapping, Architecture, Delivery) is a project framing method that structures your thinking in four phases: define the problem and objectives (Brief), map actors and risks (Mapping), choose architecture and trade-offs (Architecture), plan execution and milestones (Delivery). It avoids fuzzy projects and late feedback.
infoPage.benefit1: Clarify the problem and objectives before coding
infoPage.benefit2: Identify risks and dependencies from the start
infoPage.benefit4: Plan measurable deliverables with Definition of Done
infoPage.resonance: The BMAD wizard integrates with the Mnemosyne ecosystem: your projects are indexed by Resonance, hashtags link docs to memories, and the Brain can leverage this structured context for more relevant answers. It's framing that feeds your digital memory.
infoPage.whyAdvantage: Combining BMAD and Mnemosyne gives you an edge: you structure before acting, you document while creating, and your knowledge base stays aligned with your projects. Less documentation debt, more coherence between intention and execution.

confirm.title: Confirm creation
confirm.message: Create BMAD project "{{project}}" with current settings?
confirm.cleanupOrphansMessage: {{count}} BMAD project(s) seem deleted from disk ({{projects}}). Do you want to clean these entries from the Resonance registry?

success.registryCleanedMessage: {{count}} missing project(s) were removed from registry.
success.correctionsApplied: {{count}} correction(s) applied! Your documents have been updated.

review.description: Run a critical review before creating so the AI can enrich the documents with its recommendations.

ai.title: BMAD assistant
ai.reviewReady: Critical review is ready in the panel below.
ai.annotationPlaceholder: Add your annotation/correction for this point...
ai.noSuggestionParsed: Unable to parse usable AI suggestions.
ai.questionAssistReady: AI suggestions generated for this question.
ai.assistFallbackUsed: AI assistant unavailable, local suggestions were provided (check API keys in Settings > API Keys).
ai.notesAssistNeedAnswers: Fill at least one guided answer first to generate notes.
ai.notesAssistLocalHeader: Quick summary of the active phase
ai.notesAssistFallbackUsed: AI assistant unavailable, a local notes summary was added.
ai.hashtagsAssistFallbackUsed: AI assistant unavailable, local hashtags were added.
ai.hashtagsAssistNeedContext: Add project context first to suggest hashtags.
ai.reviewLoading: Running critical review across the full BMAD set…
ai.reviewLoadingHint: Brief, Mapping, Architecture and Delivery are sent to the reviewer model. This may take a while.
ai.reviewProcessHelpBody: Critical review analyzes the full BMAD set (Brief, Mapping, Architecture, Delivery), then highlights inconsistencies, blind spots, and corrective actions. Use annotations to accept, adjust, or challenge each point before inserting it.
ai.assistDone: AI suggestion appended to the current step.
ai.assistObjective: You are a BMAD copilot. Improve the active phase with concrete, actionable guidance.
ai.reviewObjective: You are a strict BMAD reviewer. Return only inconsistencies, blind spots, and top corrective actions.
ai.writerSystemPrompt: You are an experienced technical writer. Your goal is to take a project document plan and enrich it to make it clean, complete, structured, and ready for archiving. Write in {{language}} in standard Markdown format. Keep the user's information intact but improve style and clarity.
ai.writerPrompt:
Enrich the following Markdown document with list structures and clear rephrasings:

{{content}}
ai.reviewerSystemPrompt:
You are a demanding and experienced BMAD project reviewer. Analyze the provided BMAD project and write a critical review in Markdown format.

At the very end of your response, after your general review, you must suggest targeted corrections for fields that need improvement under the following strict text format:

=== SUGGESTIONS ===

[SUGGESTION]
CIBLE: phase.champ (Exact technical name of the field among: brief.objective, brief.problem, brief.scope, brief.notes, mapping.actors, mapping.resources, mapping.risks, mapping.notes, architecture.structure, architecture.techStack, architecture.tradeoffs, architecture.notes, delivery.milestones, delivery.validation, delivery.kpis, delivery.notes)
LABEL: Readable name of the field (e.g. Brief > Main Goal)
CRITIQUE: Brief explanation of the issue or improvement needed.
CORRECTION: The complete and rewritten text proposed to replace the current content of this field.

[SUGGESTION]
... (repeat for each suggestion)

Write in {{language}}. Be direct, constructive and pragmatic.

draft.notSavedYet: No manual local draft save yet (autosave is active).

landing.subtitle: The intelligent copilot to structure, map, and deliver your projects with precision.
landing.learnMore: Learn more about the BMAD method
landing.loadProjectDesc: Resume an existing project to update or finalize it.
landing.bmadProjectsDesc: Select a BMAD project to reload
landing.noBmadProjects: No BMAD projects found. Create a new project or finalize an existing one with BMAD.
landing.methodDesc: BMAD (Brief, Mapping, Architecture, Delivery) is a project scoping method that structures your thinking in four phases: define the problem and goals (Brief), map actors and risks (Mapping), choose the technical structure and trade-offs (Architecture), plan execution and milestones (Delivery). It avoids vague projects and late revisions.
landing.benefit1: Clarify the problem and goals before coding
landing.benefit2: Identify risks and dependencies from the start
landing.benefit4: Plan measurable deliverables with Definition of Done
landing.resonance: The BMAD wizard integrates with the Mnemosyne ecosystem: your projects are indexed by Resonance, hashtags link docs to memories, and the Brain can leverage this structured context for more relevant answers.
landing.why: Combining BMAD and Mnemosyne gives you an advantage: you structure before acting, you document while creating, and your knowledge base remains aligned with your projects.

step1.title: Project foundations
step1.subtitle: Initialize the identity information to reference this scoping.
step1.descriptionPlaceholder: Concise description of goals and expectations...

step2.title: AI & Behavior Configuration
step2.subtitle: Define how artificial intelligence will interact with the BMAD flow.
step2.telemetryAria: Sharing usage data to improve the system
step2.proactiveModeAria: AI initiative level in suggesting actions
step2.modalSubtitle: Explicitly choose the Assistant model (assistance/rephrasing) and Reviewer model (critical review).

step3.title: BMAD Flow
step3.subtitle: Fill in the Brief, Mapping, Architecture and Delivery phases.
step3.promptRephrase: Rephrase and improve this text to make it clearer and more structured. Keep the same meaning and information. Reply ONLY with the rephrased text, without introduction.

chat.welcomeMessage: Hello! I am Mnemosyne. I can help you structure your project. What is it about?

step4.title: Save & File Generation
step4.subtitle: The compiled project will be stored in your active vault. You can run an AI critical review to validate technical blind spots.
step4.createInfo: Upon creation, documents will be enriched by AI. Launch a critical review below to integrate recommendations into the docs.
step4.reviewDescription: Launch a critical review before creating so the AI can enrich documents with recommendations.
step4.reviewAria: The AI analyzes your project and provides constructive feedback
step4.applyCorrections: Apply selected corrections and return to fields (Step 3)

brief.objective: What is the main objective of this application/software?
brief.problem: What user problem are you trying to solve?
brief.scope: What are the key features of the MVP?

mapping.actors: Who are the target users, administrators, and third-party APIs?
mapping.resources: Dev team size, cloud budget, and allocated time?
mapping.risks: What are the technical or security risks?

architecture.techStack: Which languages, frameworks, and cloud services (AWS, Vercel...)?
architecture.tradeoffs: Trade-offs: speed vs quality, cost vs performance...

brief.objective: What is the main Web3 / Blockchain concept?
brief.problem: What need for trust or decentralization are you addressing?
brief.scope: Which smart contracts need to be written for the MVP?

mapping.actors: Users, validators, nodes, and oracle partners?
mapping.resources: Budget (gas fees, security audits) and dev team?

architecture.structure: Architecture of on-chain smart contracts vs off-chain services?
architecture.tradeoffs: Trade-offs of the blockchain trilemma (scalability, decentralization, security)?

delivery.validation: Audited security checks and launch criteria?
delivery.kpis: Transactions per day, TVL, active addresses?

brief.objective: What is the commercial objective or startup vision?
brief.problem: What problem or market gap in the target market are you addressing?
brief.scope: What initial products will you sell?

mapping.actors: Who are the target customers, competitors, and key suppliers?
mapping.resources: Available capital, founding team, business network?
mapping.risks: Financial, legal risks and barriers to entry?

architecture.techStack: Internal management tools (CRM, accounting, distribution channels)?

delivery.kpis: Revenue, CAC (customer acquisition cost), LTV (lifetime value)?
